area: Kernel-wide glue (ordering, evidence deadlines, parameter surface)
sources: docs/RISKS.md lines 504, 493-494 (Tie-break gaming = Low value, scoped to cheap-claim merge ties)

inputs.attackFlagText: A miner can place its own tied bid earlier in a block it mines; #25 accepts this for determinism/legibility, but the spec should say so explicitly — RISKS.md's 'tie-break gaming is low value' analysis (analysis tier) is argued for cheap-claim merge ties, not for bonded bid ties, where the tie winner does win the name.

fixture.name: alice

sameBlockTiedBids.label: bidA
sameBlockTiedBids.note: placed by the block's own miner at the lower tx index

fixture.tieCondition: both bids confirmed in the same block and otherwise tied under all auction rules (equal effective bid)
fixture.chainFacts: single block at height H; the two bids occupy tx indices 2 and 5
fixture.priorState: open auction for 'alice', no prior winner
fixture.negativeCounterpart: an impl that awards bidB (later tx order) OR treats the miner's self-placement as disallowed -> fails

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (ratified): DECISIONS #25 — same-block tied AuctionBids resolved by block transaction order. SCOPED to AuctionBid ties only (the #25 domain). DELIBERATELY does NOT extend #25 to anchor ordering (A9 / PR-16 total-order, spec-blocked) and introduces NO miner-self-placement-detection rule. RISKS.md 'tie-break gaming low value' is merge-ordering-scoped analysis (lines 493-494, 504) — it neither blocks nor overrides #25.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: the earlier-tx-order tied AuctionBid wins the name even when placed by the block's own miner; a later-ordered tied bid MUST NOT win; no self-placement-detection rule is applied
expected.rationale: The verdict — the earlier-ordered tied bid (even a miner's own) wins the name — is directly stated by ratified Decision #25 ('the bid appearing earlier in the block's transaction order wins'). The attack flag's worry (that RISKS.md's 'tie-break gaming is low value' is mis-scoped to cheap-claim merge ties, not bonded bid ties) does not change the verdict: #25 governs same-block tied AuctionBids unconditionally. A vector pinning the earlier-ordered bonded tied bid as winner is legally derivable today, independent of any provisional decision. RISKS.md confirms the 'low value' analysis is explicitly merge-ordering-scoped (lines 493-494, 504), so it neither blocks nor overrides #25.

area: Kernel-wide glue (ordering, evidence deadlines, parameter surface)
sources: docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md Item 5 B2 gate (lines 140-141, property tests over event orderings)
docs/GLOSSARY.md first-anchor-wins lines 76-79 (ordering inside a window never awards a contested name)

inputs.attackFlagText: The commutativity claim's proof lives in a research simulation (analysis-tier evidence, packages/core/src/research/delta-merge-sim.ts); the B2 property test is what turns the claim into law — if any conflicting-leaf edge falsifies it, the spec text must change by named PR, not the test soften.

fixture.disjointBatches: N accepted batches inserting pairwise-DISJOINT names (no name shared across batches)
fixture.test: evaluate the kernel over every permutation of batch arrival / merge order
fixture.expectedInvariance: one identical derived root + name state across all permutations -> accept
fixture.negativeCounterpart: an impl whose derived root for disjoint-name batches depends on arrival order -> fails the permutation sweep
fixture.orderSensitiveHalf: same-name (conflicting-leaf) batches are NOT commutative; order-sensitivity is confined to conflicting leaves and MUST NOT be assumed invariant

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate + ratified gate): DA §5 commutativity claim (candidate) + SOFTWARE_CANON B2-gate permutation-invariance obligation (ratified). SCOPED to disjoint insertions ONLY — the order-sensitive (same-name conflict) half is pinned as documented order-sensitivity, never assumed invariant. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES: same-name conflict resolution / priority ordering (first-anchor-wins, batched-path B area / spec). No #49/#50 dependency.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: disjoint batched-path insertions are permutation-invariant (one identical derived root + name state regardless of arrival/merge order); order-independence is asserted ONLY where the spec claims it (disjoint), never on conflicting leaves
expected.rationale: The order-independence verdict for disjoint batched-path insertions is claimed in DA §5 (candidate, 'Disjoint insertions commute ... the root is a function of the set of leaves, not the order or arrival time') and the permutation-invariance test obligation is mandated by the ratified B0 B2 gate ('property tests over event orderings (reorg/permutation invariance where the spec claims it)'), reinforced by GLOSSARY first-anchor-wins (candidate). A second implementer can derive 'disjoint-name batches produce one identical root regardless of permutation' from candidate+ratified text alone — the flag merely notes the proof currently sits in analysis-tier sim, which is exactly what the B2 property vector promotes to law. No dependency on #49/#50.

area: Kernel-wide glue (ordering, evidence deadlines, parameter surface)
sources: docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md L2 ownership-kernel row (line 29) and boundary rule (lines 34-42); B2 gate zero-I/O imports test (line 142); ratified fail-closed DA verdict (line 134)
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e S4 (lines 182-184, evidence in/verdict out, format = B3, never does I/O)

inputs.attackFlagText: The witnessed-evidence input format is a B3 deliverable that does not exist yet; until it is specified, any 'witnessed' parameter is a placeholder shape — a kernel API that types it as a boolean (available: yes/no) quietly re-creates the callback hole, because the boolean's provenance is unverifiable.

fixture.caseA_provenancelessBoolean: a kernel API that types the witnessed-evidence input as a bare boolean (available:true) with no verifier-checkable provenance -> reject (re-creates the evaluation-time-availability callback hole; provenance unverifiable)
fixture.caseB_absentWitness: absent / empty witnessed-evidence -> reject (fail-closed: the verdict goes against eligibility)
fixture.positiveCompanion: two honest evaluators given the SAME opaque verifier-checkable witness emit byte-identical verdicts

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (ratified): SOFTWARE_CANON boundary rule + L2 'No DB, no network, no clock' + ratified fail-closed DA verdict; DA §6e S4 (candidate) corroborates evidence-in / verdict-out / never-I/O. SCOPED to the fail-closed posture + provenance requirement, INDEPENDENT of the B3 witnessed-evidence FORMAT (spec-blocked, NOT asserted here — this vector does not define the witness shape, only that a provenance-less boolean is inadmissible and absent evidence fails closed).
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: an absent witness fails closed (against eligibility), identically for every honest evaluator; a provenance-less boolean witness is a conformance failure (it re-creates the evaluation-time-availability callback hole)
expected.rationale: Although the witnessed-evidence FORMAT is a B3 deliverable, the verdict this flag demands is the kernel's fail-closed posture on absent/unverifiable evidence — per the task gate, a flag purely about fail-closed posture on absent evidence IS derivable now even though the evidence format is B3. Ratified SOFTWARE_CANON pins it: pure predicate over witnessed inputs, 'No DB, no network, no clock', evidence layer can never override a kernel verdict, and an absent witness must fail closed (DA §6e S4 candidate corroborates). A boolean (available: yes/no) whose provenance is unverifiable re-creates the callback hole and must be a conformance failure — derivable today.
